text:
There was once a girl named Lily who lived deep within the forest. She loved exploring mysterious places and often heard strange voices calling her names somewhere deep inside.

One moonlit night, exhausted after days wandering owlhoot paths, Lily stumbled upon an ancient stone spiral. Feeling adventurous, she descended deeper until she found herself in a vast courtyard filled with statues. Among them was her favorite owl statue.

As she took admiring looks every night, strange things began happening. One owl would chatter softly—"Lily." Every time she heard it, she'd rush home feeling safer.

One sleepy afternoon, armed with nothing but her curiosity, she climbed onto owl statueoop.

Something felt off. Voices sang softer chants—a melody she knew so well.

"There you go..." someone purred as Lily spun. A thousand owls watched.

They sang secrets—

"You love them."

"Why?"

"To keep you here."

"Oh..." She blinked.

"Come here."

She obeyed.

That owl whispered heartaches lost.
